questions.question: Compare user-level threads and kernel-level threads in terms of management, performance, and blocking behavior.
keyPoints: User threads managed by library, kernel threads by OS
User threads faster to create/context switch
Blocking system call blocks all user threads but only affects the calling kernel thread

questions.question: Explain the Many-to-Many threading model with its advantages and disadvantages. Provide a real-world example where this model would be beneficial.
keyPoints: Multiplexes multiple user threads to a smaller number of kernel threads
Allows true parallelism while maintaining efficient thread management
OS can adjust number of kernel threads based on system load
Example: Web servers handling multiple concurrent requests efficiently
Comparison with other models in terms of flexibility and overhead
